text: Individual collector unit of a Dyson Swarm. Harvests stellar radiation and beams it inward as microwave energy. Cheap and expendable — the Swarm's primary power source
Orbital relay station that bounces microwave power beams around stellar occlusion zones. Required to deliver harvested energy to shadowed installations without routing it through the star's corona.

A networked constellation of silicon supercomputer satellites. No single point of failure — raw Compute is generated by sheer density of processing nodes. The backbone of any mature Swarm's cognition layer.
Quantum processing arrays stationed in the cold of deep space
column3: operating near absolute zero. Power-hungry and expensive to maintain
column4: but irreplaceable for probabilistic modeling — trajectory prediction

text: The armored core partition where the Shard's primary DMP instance runs. Built to survive what everything else cannot. If the Nexus falls
column3: the system's autonomous programming degrades rapidly. Protect it above all else.

text: A particle accelerator encircling the star at orbital distance. Generates Exotic Matter through high-energy collision events. Its energy signature is unmistakable at interstellar range — building one announces your presence to anyone watching.
Zero-gravity drydock arrays for printing new Self-Replicating Factories at scale. SRFs can replicate themselves
column5: and more precisely configured. Essential for rapid system colonization.

text: Superheated plasma containment ring used to rapidly disassemble NRA infrastructure at scale. Bypasses standard disassembly time entirely — melting billions of probes into recoverable slag in a single tick. The thermodynamic cost is severe: expect immediate Threat Level escalation.
